_comment: WC2026 Group K. Standings before this match: Portugal 4 pts, Colombia 3 (3-1 Uzbekistan), DR Congo 1 (1-1 Portugal), Uzbekistan 0 (eliminated). Colombia the quality CONMEBOL side (Luis Diaz, James, attacking) but DR Congo is NOT a minnow -- they held Portugal 1-1, organized and competitive. So this is a competitive-favorite spot, NOT a rout: applying the England-Ghana lesson, calibrate Colombia in line with the market and keep the draw/Under live. A win likely qualifies Colombia; DR Congo needs points. Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.stakes: Colombia (3 pts, beat Uzbekistan 3-1) can all but qualify with a win; DR Congo (1 pt, held Portugal 1-1) needs points to stay alive. Colombia the favourite on quality; DR Congo a competitive, organized underdog.

home.name: Colombia
notes: Quality attacking CONMEBOL side (Luis Diaz, James, Duran); beat Uzbekistan 3-1. attack 1.32.
Solid but not airtight at the back -> DR Congo can threaten, defense 0.90.
Calibrated in line with the market (~57%): DR Congo held Portugal 1-1, so this is NOT a rout spot (England-Ghana lesson). rho -0.06.

away.name: DR Congo
notes: Organized, athletic African side; held Portugal 1-1 (no minnow). attack 0.85.
Disciplined defensively, but Colombia's quality is a step up -> defense 0.97.
Needs points -> will compete; draw/Under is a live tail.

_postmatch.result: Colombia 1-0 DR Congo. Tight, low-scoring win; Colombia clean sheet, BTTS No, 1 goal total.
_postmatch.verdict: Clean hit. The favorite won by the model's #1 scoreline (1-0, 12.4%). Every read landed: Under 2.5 (53.6%), BTTS No, Colombia clean sheet (41.5%), low total (xG 2.53 -> 1 goal). The England-Ghana lesson applied UP FRONT was vindicated: DR Congo held Portugal 1-1, so I refused to overrate Colombia (kept it MEDIUM 55%, leaned Under) -- and it was indeed a tense 1-0, not a comfortable win.
_postmatch.lesson: Reinforces the organized-underdog read: when the underdog has PROVEN organization (DR Congo 1-1 Portugal), the right shape is a tight 1-0/1-1 with the draw live and Under leaned -- the opposite of the weak-side rout (Spain/Portugal 4-0). Correctly distinguishing the two archetypes is the whole game.
